title: [GRAY ZONE] TX AG v. Anzu: No response filed after 45+ days — lawsuit appears frozen
detail:
Texas AG Ken Paxton filed 7-count DTPA lawsuit in Collin County District Court on Feb 18, 2026. Anzu has not filed any public response, answer, or motion to dismiss. DroneLife attempted contact — no response. Part of coordinated TX AG action against 4 CCP-linked companies (TP-Link, Anzu, Lorex, Temu). Seeks injunctive relief, civil penalties up to $10K per violation, and court order requiring Anzu to disclose Raptor T is a rebranded DJI drone.

APRIL 4 DOCKET SEARCH: Collin County Odyssey court records system requires login (cijspub.co.collin.tx.us). Trellis.law does not surface the case in public search. No news outlet has reported ANY response, motion, hearing, or court activity since the Feb 18 filing. DroneLife attempted contact with Anzu/Warnas — no response. Multiple outlets note 'it is not immediately clear how Anzu Robotics has responded.' Under TX civil procedure, defendant typically has 20-26 days to answer after service. At 45+ days with no public activity, case is either: (a) not yet served, (b) awaiting answer with extensions, or (c) being resolved through XTI's counsel. XTI's $20M JPMorgan ABL facility lists Anzu as co-borrower — active litigation would be material to lender. Recommend manual Collin County Odyssey check.

title: [GRAY ZONE] XTI Aerospace: $20M JPMorgan ABL facility with Anzu as co-borrower despite zero products
detail: XTI acquired Anzu + Drone Nerds Nov 10, 2025 for ~$41.6M ($20M cash, $11.9M notes, $9.7M Class B units). Secured $20M JPMorgan revolving credit facility Feb 11, 2026 with Anzu as joint/several co-borrower — despite Anzu having zero products and active litigation. XTI delayed 10-K filing (April 2026) due to complex purchase price allocations. Sold Inpixon RTLS business Feb 2026. Unusual Machines invested $25M in XTI preferred stock concurrent with acquisition.

title: [GRAY ZONE] FCC blanket ban rendered ALL shell company strategies obsolete
detail: FCC Dec 22, 2025 Covered List addition went far beyond expected DJI/Autel-only action — banned ALL foreign-produced UAS and UAS critical components. DJI+Autel specifically named in Section 1709 NDAA — cannot bypass even via US manufacturing or licensing. FCC quietly revoked certain DJI/Autel authorizations issued just before the ban. First 4 drones removed from Covered List March 18, 2026 (SiFly, Mobilicom, ScoutDI, Verge) — all enterprise/inspection, none Chinese-manufactured. DoW 45-day review took 70 days.

title: [GRAY ZONE] SkyRover announces vague 5-year US manufacturing 'exploration' — still selling Chinese hardware
detail: SkyRover issued March 2026 statement: 5-year plan to 'explore US-based manufacturing and localized supply chain.' Key word: 'explore' not 'commit.' Near-term plan: 'maintain full FCC compliance' — no detail on how. S1/X1 still available at retailers. FCC filings list SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co., Ltd (Shenzhen) as manufacturer. Konrad Iturbe's OcuSync frequency bot confirmed DJI hardware signatures. SkyRover lists Hong Kong business address — still covered by FCC ban.

title: [GRAY ZONE] DJI v. FCC: Ninth Circuit Case 26-1029 — FCC moves to dismiss on ripeness
detail: DJI filed petition Feb 20, 2026 in Ninth Circuit. Docketed as Case 26-1029. Argues FCC exceeded statutory authority, failed procedures, violated Fifth Amendment. FCC moved to dismiss on ripeness grounds — bureau-level action not yet reviewed by full Commission. DJI represented by former Solicitor General. Also filed motion for reconsideration at FCC. DJI claims practical impact broader than advertised — restricting imports even outside scope. Timeline: 6-18 months for oral arguments/decision. Drone Girl predicts modified approach (audits, monitoring) rather than clean overturn.

title: [GRAY ZONE] State-level drone bans expanding: FL enforced, NY S.3259 passed, NV Jan 2024
detail: Florida: Ban enforced since April 2023. Agencies grounded $200M+ of drones. Only $25M replacement funding (12.5% coverage). Blue UAS replacements cost 2-10x more. Broward County grounded 60+ drones. Miami-Dade Fire: 'pushed back five years.' New York: S.3259/A.2237 passed 2025, aligns state procurement with federal Section 889 ban. Nevada: Banned Chinese drones for agencies Jan 2024. Arkansas: Also enacted restrictions. States enforcing with varying success — cost remains primary barrier.

title: [GRAY ZONE] DJI shell company network fully mapped — 12+ entities identified by OcuSync fingerprinting
detail: Konrad Iturbe's 'To Catch A Vibe' methodology (Oct 2025) uses DJI OcuSync frequency signatures (5745.5-5829.5 MHz) to identify rebranded DJI drones in FCC filings regardless of corporate branding. Automated GitHub bot scans daily. Identified: Cogito Tech, Spatial Hover, Lyno Dynamics, Fikaxo, Jovistar, WaveGo Tech, Knowact Robot, Skyhigh Tech, Skyany, Skyrover, and more. Evidence of coordinated network: WaveGo filing included Knowact docs, Fikaxo drone migrated to Spatial Hover overnight, DJI logos visible in Skyhigh Tech antenna diagrams. FCC Oct 2025 vote granted retroactive revocation authority specifically targeting this workaround.

title: [GRAY ZONE] FY2026 NDAA expands to Chinese ground drones, clones, and C-UAS authority
detail: $900B FY2026 NDAA passed House 312-112. Expands beyond aerial UAS to Chinese ground drones and 'clones.' SAFER SKIES Act grants state/local law enforcement C-UAS mitigation authority for events (2026 World Cup, 2028 Olympics). Mandates joint US-Taiwan drone co-development program by March 2026. Enhanced penalties: $100K/violation for unauthorized C-UAS actions.

title: [GRAY ZONE] Oregon/NASAO white paper: 467 drones grounded, $50M-$2B national exposure across 25 states
detail: Oregon Dept of Aviation compiled first multi-state accounting via NASAO. 25 state transportation depts surveyed. 467 drone airframes grounded/restricted across 23 states with explicit counts. National exposure $50M-$2B including state+local agencies and contractors. Wisconsin: 100% fleet grounded. Colorado: 90% capacity lost (16 units, 5 small drones remain). Nebraska: 86% impacted. Indiana: 85% ($400K equipment at risk). Minnesota: 84% foreign-made. Georgia: 80% ($225K). Oregon: 1 compliant drone out of 22. Idaho: $15K drone now costs $42K to replace. White paper recommends waiver until Sept 2027.

title: [GRAY ZONE] FAA ASSURE research: DJI = 96% of detected US drone platforms, Skydio = 1%
detail: ASSURE A83 2025 Annual Report (Embry-Riddle, Kansas State, NIAR, Wichita State) analyzed Remote ID telemetry through Nov 2025 at 64 monitoring locations. DJI: 96%+ of detected platforms. Skydio (the company that lobbied hardest for restrictions): slightly over 1%. All other manufacturers combined: less than 2.4%. Additionally, 58% of flights within UAS Facility Map areas exceeded altitude limits — DJI's geofencing system previously prevented exactly these incursions.

title: [GRAY ZONE] FCC Conditional Approval pipeline: 4 cleared, process running 25 days late, no Chinese systems possible
detail: FCC Conditional Approval process: submit to drones@fcc.gov → FCC forwards to DoW/DHS → individualized security assessment → approval for up to 12 months. Internal target was 45-day review. First batch took 70 days (25 late). All 4 cleared systems are enterprise/inspection from non-Chinese companies (SiFly Q12, Mobilicom SkyHopper, ScoutDI Scout 137, Verge X1). All approvals expire Dec 31, 2026. Onshoring plan required for all critical components. DJI has no practical path: 'the DoW has made no determination removing any Chinese-manufactured system, and nothing in the current framework creates a mechanism for it to do so.' Key timeline: Blue UAS + Buy American exemptions expire Jan 1, 2027.

title: [GRAY ZONE] UMAC: $150M March 2026 offering, 101st Airborne supply, PDW AM-FPV — financial bridge to post-DJI market
detail: Unusual Machines (NYSE:UMAC) priced $150M public offering March 19, 2026 at $17/share. Cash position: $103.3M. 2025 revenue: $11.2M (101% YoY). Q4: $4.9M (144% YoY). Strategic investments: $25M in XTI (34% stake at $1.492 conversion), $3.75M PDW AM-FPV order, 101st Airborne Division supply agreements. NDAA-compliant US manufacturer. Needham Buy target $20. Promoted CRO for consumer/enterprise/defense revenue. Targeting cash-flow positive by end 2026. UMAC CEO Evans: 'Drone Nerds has unprecedented opportunity to define domestic drone landscape as US intensifies ban on Chinese companies.'

title: [GRAY ZONE] Anzu government buyers identified: Little Rock PD (6 units), N. Little Rock PD, Ames PD — all now holding discontinued/litigated product
detail: 3 confirmed government buyers of Anzu Raptor T: Little Rock PD (6 units, SOAR grant funded), North Little Rock PD (Arkansas grant), Ames PD (IA). TX AG petition references police departments, surveyors, construction firms, utility operators as affected buyer categories. All are now holding a discontinued product with active litigation, no successor timeline, and DJI root key control. TX AG passthrough theory: if successful, could force state/local agencies to pause or reexamine all Anzu contracts. Officers describe Raptor T as 'truly a force multiplier' — replacement will be operationally disruptive.

title: [GRAY ZONE] TX AG 'passthrough theory' — precedent-setting test for all gray zone entities
detail: TX AG Paxton's Anzu lawsuit tests a novel legal theory: that licensing, rebranding, or supply-chain ties to a scrutinized foreign manufacturer can constitute deceptive trade practices under state consumer protection law. If successful, precedent applies to ALL gray zone entities (Cogito/SPECTA, SkyRover/Knowact, future shell companies). TX also seeks court order requiring Anzu to publicly state Raptor T is a rebranded DJI drone + TRO on data collection. Part of coordinated 4-lawsuit strategy (TP-Link, Anzu, Lorex, Temu) — Texas building a template for other AGs to follow.

title: [GRAY ZONE] Knowact corporate structure mapped: Nanshan District (DJI's home district), MAC registered Feb 2025, zero transparency
detail: SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co., Ltd (深圳市知行机器人科技有限公司) registered at Room C3-A081, Building C, Kexing Science Park, Nanshan District, Shenzhen — the same district as DJI HQ. MAC address block (68:C9:5D) registered Feb 27, 2025 — single allocation suggests purpose-built entity. FCC filings under grantee code 2BMUV. RC controllers filed under separate entity Skyhigh Tech LLC (with DJI logos in antenna diagrams). SKYROVER app on Apple App Store developed by Knowact — App Store reviews confirm it's a DJI Fly fork. Network connections: WaveGo Tech filing included Knowact docs, Fikaxo products migrated to Spatial Hover. Zero corporate transparency — no parent, no leadership, no country of origin on website.

title: [GRAY ZONE] Jan 1, 2027 firmware cliff: 3 deadlines converge — firmware waiver, Blue UAS exemption, Buy American exemption all expire
detail: Three parallel deadlines converge Jan 1, 2027: (1) FCC firmware update waiver expires — DJI/Autel/all foreign drones lose ability to receive ANY software/firmware updates including security patches, Remote ID compliance, OS compatibility; (2) Blue UAS Covered List exemption expires — non-US Blue UAS platforms lose FCC authorization pathway; (3) Buy American exemption expires — domestic-content drones lose current carveout. Impact: 80%+ of 1,800+ law enforcement agencies use DJI. Oregon white paper: 467 drones grounded across 25 states already. After Jan 1 2027 without extension, existing DJI fleets become frozen assets — no patches, no bug fixes, no OS updates. DroneXL prediction: FCC will establish formal firmware review process (source code submission) that kills update pipeline through bureaucratic friction. DSLRPros warns: firmware freeze → Remote ID compliance failure, SDK breakage, cloud tool degradation, sensor calibration loss.
